categories.name: Afterlife
categories.description: Explores ancient, biblical, and Christian views of life after death, including heaven and hell, resurrection, eternal punishment, purgatory, Jewish afterlife traditions, and journeys to the realm of the dead.

categories.name: Ancient World Context
categories.description: Provides historical background for biblical and early Christian texts, including literacy, archaeology, Greco-Roman religion, Roman power, crucifixion, social class, wealth, and material culture.

categories.name: Apocalypticism and Revelation
categories.description: Covers apocalyptic expectations in ancient Jewish, Jesus-related, Pauline, and modern contexts, with special attention to Revelation and its interpretation.

categories.name: Authorship
categories.description: Examines attribution, composition, anonymity, secretaries, sources, and named authorship of biblical and early Christian writings.

categories.name: Bart's Books
categories.description: Covers Bart Ehrman's major books, including announcements, excerpts, interviews, reader questions, reviews, and discussions of the subjects each book develops.

categories.name: Blog Features and Personal Posts
categories.description: Covers autobiographical posts, personal reflections, blog fundraising, charity updates, and the In a Nutshell series.

categories.name: Books, Teaching, and Media
categories.description: Covers trade books, textbooks, courses, interviews, videos, writing, publishing, and other ways scholarship is presented to public audiences.

categories.name: Canon and Scripture
categories.description: Explores canon formation, biblical authority, inerrancy, contradictions, scriptural interpretation, and moral problems in scripture.

categories.name: Canonical Gospels and Acts
categories.description: Covers the canonical Gospels and Acts, including composition, authorship, dating, sources, historicity, contradictions, distinctive Gospel traditions, and Acts as a narrative of early Christianity.

categories.name: Christian Origins and Expansion
categories.description: Covers Christian origins and expansion, including early Christian communities, conversion, diversity, and Christianity's rise.

categories.name: Christology
categories.description: Examines early views of Christ's identity, status, divinity, incarnation, exaltation, and representation in key texts.

categories.name: Church Fathers
categories.description: Covers church fathers and apostolic-father traditions, including Apostolic Fathers, Athanasius, Augustine, Didymus the Blind, Eusebius, Papias, and the Martyrdom of Polycarp.

categories.name: Early Church History
categories.description: Covers broad early church history, early Christian writings, early Christian diversity, Edessa, and doctrinal development such as the Trinity.

categories.name: Epistles
categories.description: Covers Pauline and General Epistles, including composition, authorship, forgery, persecution, and individual letters.

categories.name: Ethics and Social Issues
categories.description: Explores moral teaching and social issues in biblical, Jewish, early Christian, and modern interpretive contexts.

categories.name: Forgery
categories.description: Covers ancient and modern literary forgery, including Pauline, Petrine, General Epistles, and Secret Gospel of Mark cases.

categories.name: Gnosticism and Heresy
categories.description: Explores Gnosticism, heresy, proto-orthodoxy, Nag Hammadi, Marcion, and related non-canonical or textual traditions.

categories.name: Hebrew Bible and Early Judaism
categories.description: Covers Hebrew Bible texts, early Jewish groups, Jewish law, Dead Sea Scrolls, and major biblical books or traditions.

categories.name: Historical Figures and Scholars
categories.description: Covers named ancient figures, early Christian leaders, and modern scholars.

categories.name: Historical Method and Scholarship
categories.description: Covers methods used to study biblical and early Christian texts, including historical, source, redaction, dating, and public scholarship.

categories.name: Jesus
categories.description: Explores traditions about Jesus' life, teachings, family, identity, historical context, and major claims about his significance.

categories.name: Jesus' Death, Burial, and Resurrection
categories.description: Covers Jesus' arrest, trial, crucifixion, burial, empty tomb, resurrection traditions, appearances, and related interpretation.

categories.name: Manuscripts and Translations
categories.description: Covers manuscripts, manuscript discoveries and dating, textual witnesses, Bible translations, and the process of translation.

categories.name: Memory and Oral Tradition
categories.description: Explores how memory, oral transmission, eyewitness claims, and reliability shape discussion of Gospel traditions.

categories.name: Miracles
categories.description: Covers miracle traditions, healings, exorcisms, visions, apologetic claims, ancient miracle workers, and supernatural stories in biblical or related texts.

categories.name: Modern Faith and Nonbelief
categories.description: Explores belief, nonbelief, deconversion, fundamentalism, agnosticism, atheism, and suffering as a challenge to faith.

categories.name: New Testament Individuals
categories.description: Covers major figures connected with the New Testament story and its interpretation.

categories.name: Non-Canonical Texts
categories.description: Covers non-canonical gospels, apocryphal acts, apostolic writings, infancy traditions, and other early Christian texts outside the canon.

categories.name: Paul and Pauline Christianity
categories.description: Explores Paul, Pauline letters, communities, theology, ethics, conversion, Jewish law, resurrection, authorship, and later Pauline traditions.

categories.name: Persecution and Martyrdom
categories.description: Covers persecution and martyrdom traditions, including Roman persecution, apostolic martyrdom, Perpetua, Polycarp, and persecution in epistles.

categories.name: Salvation and Judgment
categories.description: Explores salvation, atonement, grace, free will, predestination, divine judgment, eternal punishment, and Pauline or Luke-Acts models.

categories.name: Suffering and Evil
categories.description: Covers suffering, evil, disasters, free will, Job, biblical interpretation of suffering, and suffering as a challenge to faith.

categories.name: Textual Criticism
categories.description: Covers textual criticism, original texts, scribal changes, variants, methods, patristic evidence, and doctrinally significant textual changes.
